sections.paragraph: ἅλυσις, εως, ἡ [ἀ-priv. (ἁ-by metathesis of aspiration), λύω, otherwise a word of uncertain orig.] chain Mk 5:3; Ac 28:20 (here a chain, perh. handcuffs, that linked Paul to the soldier guarding him [s. vs. 16] may be meant); w. focus on the state or condition imposed by being in chains = being a prisoner 2 Ti 1:16. ἐν ἅ. in chains Eph 6:20.
